mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.12% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.0% do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment exceeding 0.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) over 3 months combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals weakening consumer demand and recessionary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% over 3 months AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over same period
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 444,926 million, UNRATE at 5.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.3% signals supply-side weakness and reduced productive capacity utilization, constraining earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines do not differ significantly from baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5% signals strengthening residential investment demand and forward economic growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.5% combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days in recession-adjacent regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 5.5%, PAYEMS at 141,679 thousand
regime_hint: labor market deterioration in late-cycle environment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power (Rank IC difference < 0.01) versus UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling weakening aggregate demand and reduced earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5% in latest quarter
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 16,264.1 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-growth quarters (< 1.5% annualized) do not significantly underperform high-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05).
